_provenance.stage: Stage 1 — derived from live cards for retroactive Stage 2/3 verification
_provenance.note: This Stage 1 JSON was reconstructed from the currently-published cards so external Stage 2 (Gemini bias) and Stage 3 (Codex fact-check) reviews can verify what readers see. It is not the original analysis — the original pipeline was not run on this issue.

headline: Orang Asli Dam Resettlement Leaves 340 Families Without Clean Water
context: 340 Orang Asli families resettled for the Kelau Dam project in Pahang have been without reliable clean water since 2019, despite resettlement promises of piped supply.

cards.text: The Kelau Dam was built to provide clean water to Klang Valley.
cards.sub: 340 Orang Asli families displaced to provide that water have not had reliable clean water since resettlement.

cards.h: Resettlement houses were built without connection to the treated water network.
cards.s: Residents rely on untreated stream water. Waterborne illness rates are 4x the state average.
cards.d: Resettlement houses were built without connection to the treated water network. Residents rely on untreated stream water. Waterborne illness rates are 4x the state average.

cards.h: RM82M was allocated for the resettlement programme.
cards.s: Audit found RM31M spent on road access and administration. RM8M on housing. RM0 on water connection.
cards.d: RM82M was allocated for the resettlement programme. Audit found RM31M spent on road access and administration. RM8M on housing. RM0 on water connection.

cards.h: The dam supplies 1.1 billion litres daily to 4 million urban consumers.
cards.s: 340 displaced families need 85,000 litres daily. The dam's output is 12,900 times greater than community need.
cards.d: The dam supplies 1.1 billion litres daily to 4 million urban consumers. 340 displaced families need 85,000 litres daily. The dam's output is 12,900 times greater than community need.

cards.text: A dam that provides water to 4 million people but not to the 340 families it displaced is development by extraction.
Like demolishing a village to build a water treatment plant and then not connecting the village to the water supply
cards.sub: The dam serves 4 million people but the 340 displaced families receive neither clean water nor adequate compensation

cards.text: Displacement for infrastructure must meet minimum standards: equivalent or better services, monitored for 10 years. Anything less is state-sanctioned dispossession.

confidence: Low — derived from published cards, original brief not on disk; submit for retroactive Stage 2/3 verification
